Poetic checker run on WLC

Introduction

This page lists the 17 poetic (Three Books) WLC 4.22 verses the PLY accent grammar cannot parse cleanly (13 missing-silluq, 4 NO_PARSE). Use the filter below to narrow the list.

Each verse falls into one of two documented kinds:

  • “missing silluq,” where the sof pasuq arrives with no silluq, recovered into an ERROR-leaf tree (structure preserved).
  • “NO_PARSE,” a hierarchy-violating L anomaly for which no valid tree exists.

Each verse shows its pointed-Hebrew text (the verse-final word highlighted for missing-silluq cases), its Michigan-Claremont source body, and a WLC-vs-MAM-simple disjunctive compare: the scanner's disjunctive skeleton against the MAM oracle's. The per-verse summary is mechanically auto-derived from that compare and labelled tentative — it is not a hand-vetted attribution. See doc/PLAN-poetic-accent-grammar.md for the full taxonomy.

Oddball kind
Book
MAM compare

Psalms 17:14

🔗 Kind: NO_PARSE (hierarchy-violating L anomaly).

Mwd | UXLC

Auto-derived summary (tentative): WLC and MAM-simple read the same disjunctive skeleton, yet the verse does not parse — the anomaly is structural, not a WLC/MAM divergence.

ממת֥ים ידך֨׀ יהו֡ה ממת֬ים מח֗לד חלק֥ם בחיים֮ וצפונך֮ תמל֪א ב֫טנ֥ם ישבע֥ו בנ֑ים והנ֥יחו י֝תר֗ם לעולליהֽם׃

MAMLEGARMEH PAZER REVIA_GADOL TSINNOR TSINNOR OLEH_WEYORED ATNAX REVIA_MUGRASH SILLUQ

🔗 Kind: NO_PARSE (hierarchy-violating L anomaly).

Mwd | UXLC

Auto-derived summary (tentative): Relative to the MAM-simple oracle, WLC reads legarmeh where MAM reads oleh weyored.
